prerequisites: A running SAP HANA database (on-premise SAP HANA Platform or SAP HANA Cloud) reachable from where pm runs.

The host name or IP address of the SAP HANA system and its SQL (JDBC) port. For a tenant database on SAP HANA Platform the SQL port follows the 3<instance>NN pattern (for example 3<NN>15 / 3<NN>13); SAP HANA Cloud listens on port 443.
A SAP HANA database user (the 'username') with a password, granted SELECT on the schemas and tables you intend to read. Use GRANT to assign object or schema privileges to this user.
A cursor field: pm requires the 'cursor' value to track incremental reads.
If connecting over TLS, the server's SSL certificate so the client can validate the encrypted connection (maps to encryption.ssl_certificate).
If the database is not directly reachable, an SSH bastion host plus either an SSH private key or an SSH user password for the tunnel (maps to tunnel_method).

authMethods.name: Username and password (SAP HANA database user)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a SAP HANA database user name (the 'username' field) and its 'password'. This is SAP HANA's standard internal user/password authentication over JDBC. The user must have SELECT privileges on the target schemas and tables.

authMethods.name: TLS/SSL encrypted connection
authMethods.summary: Configure the 'encryption' setting to connect over TLS. When validating the server, supply the server's SSL certificate via 'encryption.ssl_certificate'. SAP HANA JDBC enables encryption via the encrypt connection property and validates the server certificate against a trust store.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel
authMethods.summary: Optionally reach the database through an SSH bastion using 'tunnel_method'. Provide either an SSH private key ('tunnel_method.ssh_key') or an SSH user password ('tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password'). This secures network transport to the host and is not a SAP HANA database credential.

setupSteps.title: Create a SAP HANA database user
setupSteps.body: In SAP HANA, create a database user for pm and set its password. Grant it SELECT on the schemas and tables you want to replicate using the GRANT statement (object or schema privileges). Use these as the 'username' and 'password' fields.

setupSteps.title: Identify host and SQL port
setupSteps.body: Set 'host' to the SAP HANA host name or IP. Set 'port' to the SQL (JDBC) port: on SAP HANA Platform a tenant database uses the 3<instance-number>NN port range (for example 3NN15/3NN13); SAP HANA Cloud uses port 443. Optionally set 'database' to the target tenant database and 'schemas' to limit which schemas are read.

setupSteps.title: Configure encryption
setupSteps.body: Set the required 'encryption' field to choose how the connection is secured. For TLS, the SAP HANA JDBC client uses the encrypt property and validates the server certificate; provide the server certificate in 'encryption.ssl_certificate' so the connection can be verified.

setupSteps.title: Configure network access (tunnel)
setupSteps.body: Set the required 'tunnel_method'. For a direct connection choose the no-tunnel option. To connect through an SSH bastion, provide the bastion details and either 'tunnel_method.ssh_key' (private key) or 'tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password'.

setupSteps.title: Set the cursor and optional tuning fields
setupSteps.body: Provide the required 'cursor' field used for incremental reads. Optionally set 'jdbc_url_params' to pass extra JDBC connection properties, and adjust 'check_privileges', 'concurrency', 'filters', and 'checkpoint_target_interval_seconds' as needed.
